summary: First iteration end-to-end testing of BrowsEMS landing page. Backend (13/13 pytest cases) and frontend (all critical flows) PASS. Health, newsletter (with idempotent duplicate handling + 422 invalid email), contact (with 422 missing fields), booking (with 422 missing required) all verified. ObjectId leak protection verified across all list endpoints. Frontend: hero video element present with correct src, 3 hero text states with cross-fade opacity, scroll-scrub progress indicator updates from 00->69 as user scrolls, EN/ES language toggle with localStorage persistence (key=browsems_locale), newsletter/contact/booking forms POST to backend and clear inputs + show sonner success toast ('You're on the list. Welcome to the family'). Mobile (390x844) shows hamburger toggle, opens menu correctly, and hero falls back to muted autoplay+loop video.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated FastAPI on_event('shutdown') — consider lifespan handler in future refactor (non-blocking, FastAPI still supports it).
BookingCreate.preferred_date is a free string (min 8, max 20); could be tightened with regex/date validation in future to enforce YYYY-MM-DD.
Newsletter idempotency is based on raw email equality (case-sensitive). Consider lowercasing email before lookup/insert to avoid duplicate records for 'A@x.com' vs 'a@x.com'.
ScrollVideoHero useScrub is fixed at mount (useState(detectEnv)) so a desktop user resizing into mobile width won't trigger autoplay fallback until reload — acceptable trade-off but worth noting.
test_credentials: N/A — public landing page, no auth.
seed_data_creation: Pytest created several TEST_-prefixed newsletter/contact/booking records in MongoDB (idempotent, safe to ignore or purge manually).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and run cleanly via `pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. BASE_URL is sourced from frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Frontend Playwright flows covered: hero video presence, scroll scrub progress, hero text states 0..2, EN/ES toggle + localStorage, newsletter/contact/booking submit + toast, mobile hamburger + video autoplay fallback. Hero video is 39MB; in headless we did not assert playback, only element + src + handlers, as instructed.
